page: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors. It features 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz with a dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is anchored by a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU evaluations, as well as Passmark CPU tests, including encryption and physics simulations. Memory performance is exceptionally strong, with small-block cached read bandwidth reaching 25,059.68 GB/sec and large-block uncached write bandwidth at 314.43 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations, which reached 331.8 million operations per second.

From a resource density perspective, the m7a.metal-48xl offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring massive parallel processing and high-speed DDR5 memory without virtualization overhead. However, the lack of local NVMe storage represents a key tradeoff, requiring reliance on network-attached storage for persistent data. While prompt processing for large language models ranks in the top tier, text generation speeds for small and large models fall into the average tier, indicating a potential bottleneck for certain real-time generative AI tasks.

This bare-metal configuration is highly suited for high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, intensive database operations, static web serving at scale, and memory-bound parallel processing tasks. It is also well-suited for the prompt-processing phase of LLM inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage. In benchmark testing, the server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and Redis operations, alongside exceptional memory read and write bandwidth. The absence of local storage requires network-attached storage, representing a clear architectural tradeoff. This server is highly suited for compute-heavy workloads, massive database operations, high-performance computing, and the prompt-processing phase of language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core compute and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores with DDR5 memory for demanding parallel computing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (4.0 GB per core)
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, Redis, and software compilation benchmarks
Suited for high-performance computing, large databases, and LLM prompt processing
